name: Fetch joke and research comedian
description: Fetch a random joke from the official joke API, research the comedian who originated that style, and save results to joke_research.txt

tasks.name: Fetch random joke from API
tasks.description: You MUST call http_request to GET https://official-joke-api.appspot.com/random_joke. Output the FULL raw response body verbatim (do NOT summarize as 'joke was fetched'). Downstream tasks need the actual joke text. Expected output format: JSON object with 'setup' and 'punchline' fields.
tasks.success_criteria: Output contains raw JSON response with 'setup' and 'punchline' fields from the API

tasks.name: Search for comedian and joke style
tasks.description: You MUST call web_search (or WebSearch) to find information about the comedian or comedy style. First, extract the joke text from task_001's output. Then search for who originated this type of joke or similar jokes. Output the FULL raw search results verbatim (URLs, titles, snippets). Do NOT summarize as 'results were found' - downstream tasks need the actual data. If no specific comedian is found, say 'comedian not found' and describe the joke style instead.
tasks.success_criteria: Output contains search results with at least one URL starting with https:// OR states 'comedian not found' with style description

tasks.name: Write joke and research to file
tasks.description: You MUST call file_write to create joke_research.txt. Extract the joke (setup and punchline) from task_001's output and the research findings from task_002's output. Format the file with: 1) The joke text (setup and punchline), 2) A separator line, 3) The research findings about the comedian/style. Use clear formatting with headers.
tasks.success_criteria: File joke_research.txt is created containing both the joke text and research findings
